audit_items.proof_trace: lutil_sasl_freedefs relies solely on assert(defs!=NULL) (L53) to guard against NULL dereference at L55; assert is a no-op in release/NDEBUG builds. Two callers — clients/tools/common.c:1526 and clients/tools/ldapvc.c:387 — call lutil_sasl_freedefs(defaults) unconditionally without checking the NULL return from lutil_sasl_defaults, creating a reachable null dereference when ber_memalloc fails. Additionally, interaction::L195 dereferences defaults->resps without a null check, reachable when flags==LDAP_SASL_INTERACTIVE and defaults==NULL (same OOM path) and the user enters non-empty input.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker-induced or system-level memory exhaustion causes ber_memalloc to return NULL inside lutil_sasl_defaults; clients/tools/common.c passes the NULL return directly to the SASL bind and then to lutil_sasl_freedefs, crashing the LDAP client process. With LDAP_SASL_INTERACTIVE mode, the same NULL defaults pointer reaches interaction::L195 and causes a null dereference on defaults->resps.
